content:
I think everyone has over-complicated this issue or doesn't understand what I'm talking about. I used to use Outlook back in the day, and I never used the preview pane, as this was susceptible to attack years ago (basically it opened the emails whether you wanted them open or not). I would double click an email and it would open in its own window. When I converted over to Thunderbird, I set it up the same way...no issues.

With a little more looking around in the Mozilla site, here's what I found: 1. Click on Inbox (or probably anywhere in the Thunderbird window). 2. Press the ALT key (a toolbar appears near the top of the window). 3. Select "View'. 4. Select "Sort by". 5. Select "Unthreaded" ("Threaded" was selected by default, I assume).

Now when I double-click an email, the entire email thread (or chain or whatever you want to call it) is in the single window, just like the good old days. This seems more like "Threaded" than "Unthreaded" to me, but at least I got it to work.

content: No, you can't. Thunderbird is an email program that runs on your local computer and interacts with external email services like Gmail, Comcast, AOL, Plusnet, or (resumably) Greenby . Thunderbird doesn't offer email hosting services. So, you'll have to find another email host on your own. Once you do that, you will almost certainly be able to use Thunderbird to manage the email on that account.

content: A .vcs file is a legacy calendar format officially known as a vCalendar file. While it has largely been superseded by the more modern .ics (iCalendar) format, it is still used by older software and some mobile devices to exchange scheduling information. Modern versions of Thunderbird only support the .ics (iCalendar) format for importing events. The .vcs (vCalendar 1.0) format is considered obsolete, and direct support for it has been removed.

content:
Right click the folder, select compact. EMail with a 1969 date are generally a sign the storage file is corrupted compacting should clear that. The only question is always what is lost when the corruption is removed so backup any email in the folder you want to keep before you do the compact.

You might also want to be looking for what caused the corruption in the first place. This is often an antivirus product that is scanning the file while Thunderbird is trying to write to it. Any activity that could cause contention is suspect, but by far the most common on windows is antivirus. The second is cloud backups to those that moved the mail profile to location like documents where it is not treated the same as it is in it's default location.

content:
Přidání nového sloupce do seznamu zpráv a zjišťování „offline dostupnosti“ sahá hluboko do interní logiky Thunderbirdu. To už není jednoduchý informační doplněk.

Současné doplňky pro Thunderbird používají MailExtensions / WebExtensions, které mají omezená API. Informace o offline stavu zprávy navíc nejsou vždy doplňkům přímo dostupné.

Proč se doplněk nenainstaluje: Typicky jde o chybný manifest.json, použití nepodporovaných API nebo funkci, kterou Thunderbird z bezpečnostních důvodů nepovoluje.

Laik bez hlubší znalosti JavaScriptu a specifik Thunderbirdu na tohle prakticky nemá šanci. Požadavek je výrazně nad úrovní začátečníka a částečně i nad možnostmi současného API.

There are at least three ways:

content:
The last entry in that list is the last location you moved or copied something to. It's intended to make subsequent moves easier to do with fewer clicks.

You don't mention what version of Thunderbird you're using. The Current version 147.0.1 has the new option to Reset Folder Order, which will alphabetize them, though it leaves the special folders (Inbox, Drafts, Sent, Spam, Trash) at the top. The option can be found by right-clicking on the account name (see attached image.)

Version 140.7.1esr does not have this option. You can reorder them manually by dragging and dropping, but that's a delicate operation and it's easy to accidentally drop it into another folder rather than between them.

content:
I have to ask to what purpose these check box would be used. They are supplied as an accessibility aid for those that struggle with certain mobility issues, but they server no purpose to someone using windows and a mouse. None at all. The Shift and Ctrl keys in conjunction with the mouse provide all the multiple selection options anyone could want especially in conjunction with the Ctrl+A select all shortcut.

I find almost everyone asking for check boxes is unaware of the features for multiple selection that come with their operating system and are supported by Thunderbird.
creator: MattAuSupport

content:
I'm neither unaware of using shift or ctrl in combination with a mouse, but there are others who can't due to mobility issues and there are also others who simply don't (want) use mouse and keyboard in combination (for what reason ever). And frankly speaking, when selecting multiple messages, just mark it with a checkbox is sometimes more convenient than using the keyboard in combination.

In table view it's available, in card view not? That does not make any sense to me.

content:
I should mention I have three email accounts that Thunderbird was handling for years and years without issue.

Account 1: user1@verizon.net Account 2: user2@gmail.com Account 3: user3@verizon.net

Verizon accounts are handled by AOL.

I have no issues with Account 2 and 3, just Account 1.

Here's what I've done so far.

Shut down Norton. Uninstalled Thunderbird and deleted the AppData>Thunderbird directory. Reinstalled Thunderbird.

Recreated Account 1. Same issue as before.

Aha, then there must be an issue with some emails on AOL regarding account 1.

I signed on to AOL, deleted any emails that I really didn't need including the Spam and Trash folders.

Now Thunderbird seems fine.

content:
Here are the steps:

  1. install thunderbird if not already installed, downloaded from thunderbird.net
  2. open the backup profile that you posted in Windows File explorer, highlight all and COPY all to clipboard
  3. in thunderbird, click help>troubleshootinginformation
  4. scroll to 'profile folder' and click 'open folder'
  5. now, paste contents of exported profile, overlaying contents of profile.
  6. if there is a compatibilitie.ini file, delete it
  7. restart thunderbird
creator: davidsk

content:
I just fixed this following the below instructions on a Google search for anyone else having this problem

Open the Registry Editor by typing regedit in the Run dialog (Windows Key + R) and clicking OK. Navigate to HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\WOW6432Node\Microsoft\Windows Messaging Subsystem. In the right-hand panel, check if a String Value (REG_SZ) named MAPI exists. If it does not exist, right-click in the blank space, select New > String Value, and name it MAPI. Double-click the MAPI string value and enter 1 in the Value data field, then click OK. For comprehensive MAPI support, some sources also suggest adding a MAPIX string value with a value of 1 in the same location. Close the Registry Editor and restart the application (or your computer) for the changes to take effect.
creator: receiving1
